meta.name: Light of the Crystals (FF1 refined)
meta.source: Reimagined from ff1-theme.json — adds surface depth + Crystal identity
meta.tokenConvention: Every color key maps to a CSS custom property named --<key>. e.g. "accent" -> var(--accent). The four crystal-* tokens are extra identity colors; wire them up when the theme system supports custom tokens.
meta.notExportedYet: Non-color tokens. The faceted-gem look really wants subtle gradients on panels and a luminous border-glow (box-shadow) — neither is expressible as a flat color token yet. For a strict NES feel keep --radius: 0; for a 'remake' feel allow rounded corners. The crystal-earth/fire/water/wind tokens are not yet referenced by styles.css.

bg.desc: Cavern of the Crystals — deep indigo void instead of flat black

bg-soft.desc: Sticky header / search elevation, a step up from the void

panel.desc: Crystalline navy menu panel — now distinct from the background

line.desc: Crystal-lit steel blue border — keeps the iconic frame without stark white

line-soft.desc: Dim navy divider for inner rows and sections

text.desc: Moonlit white with a cool crystal tint

muted.desc: Hazy blue-gray for labels and captions

faint.desc: Dim blue-gray for tertiary text / idle indicators

accent.desc: Water Crystal blue — FF's signature accent and focus glow

accent-dk.desc: Deeper crystal blue — focus border / active sort header

gold.desc: Crystal light & Gil — section titles and treasure highlights

done-dk.desc: Darker shade of the restored state

todo.desc: Dormant crystal slate — waiting to be restored

excluded.desc: Petrified stone — muted violet-gray, set aside

danger.desc: Fire Crystal red — critical state / destructive actions
